seo.title: Binance BTC/USDT Funding Rate — 7,087 Settlements Analyzed | Zirodelta
seo.description: BTC/USDT funding rate on Binance: 12.23% annualized across 7,087 settlements (Sep 2019–Mar 2026). 86.5% positive rate frequency. Real data from 9.4M+ funding rate records.

content.intro: BTC/USDT on Binance is the most liquid perpetual futures market in crypto. We have analyzed 7,087 individual funding rate settlements spanning September 2019 to March 2026 — over 6.5 years of continuous data from our 9.4M+ settlement record dataset. The average funding rate is +0.0112% per 8-hour settlement, translating to 12.23% annualized yield for delta-neutral short positions. 86.5% of all settlements pay shorts (positive rate), making this one of the most consistent funding rate arbitrage opportunities in the market.
content.how_funding_works: Binance calculates BTC/USDT funding rates every 8 hours at 00:00, 08:00, and 16:00 UTC using a premium/discount index between the perpetual price and spot price. When the perp trades at a premium (bullish sentiment), the rate is positive and longs pay shorts. When it trades at a discount, shorts pay longs. Binance clamps the rate between -0.30% and +0.30% per settlement. Across our 7,087 recorded settlements, the average is +0.0112% — heavily skewed toward positive, meaning short positions have a structural edge.
content.why_this_pair: BTC/USDT on Binance is the gold standard for funding rate arbitrage because of three factors our data confirms: (1) Highest liquidity — over $20B daily volume means negligible slippage on entry/exit. (2) Most consistent positive rates — 86.5% positive frequency is the highest among BTC pairs across all exchanges we track. (3) Deepest history — 7,087 settlements since Sep 2019 gives us 6.5 years of validated patterns. No other funding rate data source offers this depth of analysis.

sections.heading: Historical Funding Rate Data (7,087 Settlements)
sections.body: Our analysis of 7,087 Binance BTC/USDT settlements reveals an average rate of +0.0112% per 8h cycle (12.23% annualized). The median rate is +0.0100%, confirming the distribution skews positive. Rates range from -0.30% to +0.30% (Binance clamps at these extremes). Out of 7,087 settlements, 6,127 (86.5%) were positive and only 960 (13.5%) were negative — meaning shorts collect funding roughly 6 out of every 7 settlement periods.

sections.heading: Monthly Rate Trends (Last 12 Months)
sections.body: Funding rates show clear seasonality. July 2025 was the strongest month with 100% positive settlements and 8.58% annualized return. January 2026 followed strong at 5.39% annualized (95.7% positive). However, March 2026 has turned negative at -1.46% annualized with only 36.6% positive settlements — reflecting the current bearish market sentiment. This cyclical pattern is exactly what our engine exploits: enter during positive rate regimes, exit when rates decay below threshold.

sections.heading: Cross-Exchange Comparison: Where BTC Funding Pays Most
sections.body: Comparing BTC/USDT across 4 major exchanges using our dataset: Hyperliquid leads with 16.12% annualized (24,331 settlements at 1h intervals, 88.7% positive). Binance follows at 13.96% annualized (19,214 settlements). Bybit delivers 11.77% annualized (10,635 settlements, 73% positive). KuCoin trails at 7.62% annualized (8,631 settlements, 68.5% positive). The key insight: Hyperliquid's 1-hour funding interval captures rates 3x more frequently than Binance's 8-hour cycle, compounding the advantage.

sections.heading: Arbitrage Execution: How Zirodelta Captures This
sections.body: Zirodelta's engine automates the delta-neutral strategy on BTC/USDT: buy 1 BTC spot, short 1 BTC perpetual. Net exposure: zero. Every 8 hours when the rate is positive (86.5% of the time), the short position collects funding. Our engine routes across exchanges — if Binance rates compress, it checks Bybit, Hyperliquid, and KuCoin for better rates. Smart venue routing ensures we always capture the best available spread. Historical backtest over 6.35 years: 19.55% CAGR, Sharpe 3.74, max drawdown 3.48%.

faq.question: What is the average BTC/USDT funding rate on Binance?
faq.answer: Based on our analysis of 7,087 settlements from September 2019 to March 2026, the average BTC/USDT funding rate on Binance is +0.0112% per 8-hour cycle, equivalent to approximately 12.23% annualized. The median rate is +0.0100%, and 86.5% of all settlements are positive (shorts collect).

faq.question: How often is the BTC/USDT funding rate positive on Binance?
faq.answer: 86.5% of the time. Out of 7,087 recorded settlements, 6,127 were positive and 960 were negative. Some months like July 2025 saw 100% positive settlements. Bear market months like March 2026 can drop to 36.6% positive.

faq.question: Which exchange has the highest BTC funding rate?
faq.answer: Hyperliquid leads with 16.12% annualized across 24,331 settlements, followed by Binance at 13.96%, Bybit at 11.77%, and KuCoin at 7.62%. However, Hyperliquid settles every 1 hour vs Binance's 8 hours, so the per-settlement rate is much smaller but compounds more frequently.

faq.question: Can funding rate arbitrage on BTC/USDT lose money?
faq.answer: The delta-neutral strategy (buy spot + short perp) has near-zero price risk. Losses come from: (1) negative funding periods where you pay instead of collect (13.5% of BTC/USDT settlements on Binance), (2) trading fees on entry/exit, and (3) basis risk during extreme volatility. Our 6.35-year backtest shows a maximum drawdown of just 3.48% with a Sharpe ratio of 3.74.

faq.question: How much capital do I need for BTC/USDT funding arbitrage?
faq.answer: You need enough to buy spot BTC and maintain margin for the perpetual short. Practically, $1,000+ is viable on Binance (minimum order sizes). Professional strategies typically deploy $10,000-$500,000 per pair, sized by open interest caps to avoid market impact.
